summary: The diff implements frontend username resolution for post/comment authors (with fallback labels and avatar initials) via a new React Query hook, and adds frontend profile-gating for creating posts. However, backend enforcement (trap on missing profile) is not evidenced in the provided backend diff excerpt, and several unrelated UI/UX/theme changes appear to have been made beyond the request scope.

missing_requirements.requirement: Enforce on the backend that a user must have an existing profile before they can create a post or add a comment (createPost/addComment trap with clear English message when userProfiles.containsKey(caller) is false).
missing_requirements.reason: The provided `backend/main.mo` excerpt does not show any changes to `createPost` or `addComment`, nor any profile existence checks/traps. Because the backend file is truncated and the summary does not explicitly mention this enforcement, implementation cannot be confirmed from the diff evidence.
evidence: backend/main.mo (truncated excerpt shows types/helpers but not createPost/addComment changes)

missing_requirements.requirement: Ensure the frontend prevents posting/commenting when the current user has not created a profile, aligning UI with backend enforcement (including handling backend 'profile required' errors as readable English messages).
missing_requirements.reason: PostComposer includes a profile check and English toast, but the acceptance criteria also require that the existing mandatory profile setup modal continues to block interaction globally (no routes/pages behind it) and that comment submission is disabled/gated in edge cases. The diff does not include evidence of App-level modal gating behavior or the comment-submit disablement behavior (FeedPostCard is truncated), so full REQ-13 compliance cannot be verified.
evidence: frontend/src/components/FeedPostCard.tsx (truncated; cannot confirm comment submit disablement/gating)
frontend/src/App.tsx (not shown as modified; cannot confirm global modal gating behavior)
